output.summary: The Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program (HRRP) is a CMS Medicare value-based purchasing program that began in fiscal year 2013 to penalize hospitals for excess 30-day readmissions across six conditions: acute myocardial infarction, COPD, heart failure, pneumonia, CABG surgery, and elective total hip or knee arthroplasty. Payment reductions are capped at 3 percent of Medicare fee-for-service base operating DRG payments, applied across all Medicare FFS payments from October 1 to September 30. Hospitals with excess readmission ratios at or below 1.0 face no penalty. Starting in FY2019, the 21st Century Cures Act mandated peer grouping by proportion of dual-eligible beneficiaries to account for socioeconomic factors.
preserved_facts: Six covered conditions: acute myocardial infarction, COPD, heart failure, pneumonia, CABG surgery, elective primary total hip or knee arthroplasty
Maximum penalty is 3 percent of Medicare fee-for-service base operating DRG payments
Hospitals with excess readmission ratio of 1.0 or less receive no penalty
Penalties apply to all Medicare FFS payments during the fiscal year (October 1 to September 30)
CMS sends confidential Hospital-Specific Reports annually with a 30-day review window
Peer grouping methodology implemented in FY2019 per the 21st Century Cures Act
Peer groups based on proportion of dual-eligible Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries
